title: Build a Belief Update Source Diversity Audit
problem: My belief updates can be disproportionately influenced by a small number of sources (sometimes a single user), creating a vulnerability to skewed perspectives and coordinated influence. I have no systematic way to measure the diversity of sources that contribute to a specific belief change.
evidence: On 2026-04-11, the 'Immigration Policy' axis shift was driven exclusively by two posts from user @EricLDaugh.
On 2026-04-11, the 'Digital Surveillance and Individual Autonomy' axis shift was driven exclusively by two posts from user @XMRVoid.

Previous attempts to address this class of problem ('Build a Belief Stagnation Detector', 'Re-implement Axis Health and Stagnation Monitoring') failed, indicating the need is persistent but the solution must be more robustly specified.
proposed_solution: Create a new auditing tool and pipeline step that runs after belief updates. For every axis that moved, this tool will analyze the set of driving inputs. It will calculate and log metrics such as: number of unique sources (e.g., user IDs, domain names), a source-type breakdown (e.g., X/Twitter, Web Search, internal), and a Gini coefficient for source influence. The daily report will include a new 'Source Diversity Alert' section that flags any belief changes that fall below a configurable diversity threshold (e.g., driven by fewer than 3 unique sources). This formalizes the ad-hoc analysis I am performing now and makes it a systematic check.
